skill.name: vue-best-practices
skill.description: Vue 3 best-practice guidance for TypeScript, vue-tsc, Volar, Pinia, Vite, SSR, typed routing, and component library patterns.
skill.summary: Apply focused Vue 3, TypeScript, Volar, and Vite guidance during coding, review, and refactoring.
skill.author: antfu

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are mostly markdown code fences, Vue and TypeScript examples, relative paths, and reference URLs. No prompt injection, credential access, data exfiltration, or executable skill script was found. A low-risk documentation issue remains because one troubleshooting block uses rm -rf to clear framework caches.

low_findings.title: Destructive Cache Cleanup Command in Documentation
low_findings.description: A Nuxt HMR troubleshooting block suggests rm -rf .nuxt node_modules/.vite before reinstalling dependencies. This is not executed by the skill and targets build caches, but assistants should add confirmation and directory checks before recommending destructive shell commands. Confidence: 0.78. Confidence reasoning: The command is clearly present in a bash documentation block, but its scope is limited to Vue/Nuxt cache directories and there is no automatic execution path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is clearly present in a bash documentation block, but its scope is limited to Vue/Nuxt cache directories and there is no automatic execution path.

low_findings.title: Static Findings Are Predominantly Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged many code fences, JSON comments, TypeScript snippets, package install commands, relative paths, and reference links as command, filesystem, network, or cryptography indicators. Manual review found these are instructional examples rather than hidden executable behavior. Confidence: 0.9. Confidence reasoning: Reviewed representative flagged files including SKILL.md, SYNC.md, codeactions-save-performance.md, module-resolution-bundler.md, duplicate-plugin-detection.md, and hmr-vue-ssr.md; the content is documentation and examples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed representative flagged files; the content is documentation and examples, not hidden executable behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: rm -rf Shell Command Example
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes a bash example that removes .nuxt and node_modules/.vite cache directories. This is a recognizable destructive shell pattern, but it is presented as a troubleshooting step rather than embedded executable code. Confidence: 0.82. Confidence reasoning: The dangerous pattern is literal and easy to verify, while the surrounding context limits it to cache cleanup documentation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The dangerous pattern is literal and easy to verify, while the surrounding context limits it to cache cleanup documentation.

content.value_statement: Vue 3 projects often fail in subtle places: template types, wrapper props, Volar configuration, SSR HMR, and test setup. This skill gives targeted patterns that help Claude, Codex, and Claude Code fix those issues with current Vue tooling.
actual_capabilities: Explains strict template checking with vueCompilerOptions for vue-tsc projects.
Shows how to extract props, emits, slots, and exposed types from Vue single-file components.
Guides Volar, vue-language-server, module resolution, and editor migration fixes.
Provides Pinia testing patterns for createTestingPinia, spies, state setup, and resets.
Covers typed routes, defineModel edge cases, withDefaults unions, and directive comments.
Documents Vite and Nuxt SSR HMR troubleshooting patterns for Vue applications.
limitations: It is a documentation skill, not an executable linter or automated code transformer.
Examples may need adjustment for each project structure, package manager, and Vue version.
Some guidance depends on current Vue Language Tools and Volar behavior.
It does not replace project tests, type checks, or dependency review.

use_cases.title: Review Vue Component Types
use_cases.description: Audit Vue components for weak prop extraction, missing strict template checks, and unsafe wrapper attribute patterns.

use_cases.title: Fix Editor and Build Tooling
use_cases.description: Diagnose Volar, vue-language-server, moduleResolution, Vite, and SSR HMR issues after dependency upgrades.

use_cases.title: Stabilize Vue Test Suites
use_cases.description: Apply reliable Pinia mocking and store reset patterns for Vitest component and store tests.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Vue Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Vue 3 component for TypeScript and vue-tsc issues. Focus on props, emits, slots, template types, and simple fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Strict Template Errors
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me enable or repair strictTemplates in this Vue project. Explain which tsconfig should change and how to handle data-* attributes.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose Tooling Upgrade Breakage
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced Volar or Vite migration task
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these Vue tooling errors after upgrading Volar, vue-language-server, @vue/tsconfig, or Vite. Recommend minimal configuration changes.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor a Component Library Pattern
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced architecture and type reuse task
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this Vue component library pattern. Preserve runtime behavior while improving wrapper props, fallthrough attributes, component type extraction, and template safety.

output_examples.input: A wrapper button component forwards attributes but has weak TypeScript support.
output_examples.output: The response identifies fallthrough attribute risks, recommends the relevant Vue compiler option, and outlines a typed wrapper strategy.

output_examples.input: A project reports module resolution errors after updating @vue/tsconfig.
output_examples.output: The response compares bundler and node module resolution, checks TypeScript version assumptions, and suggests the least disruptive config fix.

output_examples.input: Pinia store tests fail because actions are not mocked correctly.
output_examples.output: The response explains createTestingPinia setup, createSpy requirements, initial state placement, and reset practices between tests.

best_practices: Run vue-tsc and project tests after applying configuration or type changes.
Match guidance to the project Vue, Volar, TypeScript, Vite, and Pinia versions.
Confirm destructive cleanup commands before running them, especially outside disposable caches.
anti_patterns: Applying every rule globally without checking the project version and failure mode.
Suppressing template type errors with directive comments before fixing the underlying type issue.
Using shell cleanup commands in the wrong working directory or without reviewing the paths.

faq.question: Does this skill execute code?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples for Vue projects. Users or agents decide which commands or edits to apply.

faq.question: Which Vue versions does it target?
faq.answer: It focuses on Vue 3, with some guidance specific to Vue 3.5, Volar 3, and current Vue Language Tools.

faq.question: Can it fix vue-tsc strict template errors?
faq.answer: It can guide configuration and code changes for common strict template errors, but the project should still run vue-tsc.

faq.question: Does it support Pinia tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes patterns for createTestingPinia, createSpy, initial state, action behavior, and test reset handling.

faq.question: Are the reference URLs required at runtime?
faq.answer: No. The URLs are documentation references. The skill does not make network requests itself.

faq.question: Why is the security risk low?
faq.answer: The audit found documentation examples and links, not executable malware. One cache cleanup command needs user confirmation before use.
